text: Plumb must be installable via both pip and uv using the package name 'plumb-dev' on PyPI with CLI command 'plumb'
The system must support two formats for linking tests to requirements: comment-based markers using '# plumb:req-XXXXXXXX' format and function name-based linking using 'test_req_XXXXXXXX_' format
The system must filter for spec-relevant content during decision extraction in both conversation analysis and diff analysis methods
The system must implement semantic similarity checking to handle duplicate or similar decisions effectively
Classification tasks must not generate reasoning traces to maintain optimal performance
LLM-based deduplication must utilize Claude Haiku 4.5 as the designated model
The deduplicate_decisions() function must accept a use_llm parameter that defaults to False
Plumb must integrate with git hooks to automatically trigger workflows based on repository events
Plumb must store all state in a '.plumb/' folder at the root of the user's repository that should be committed to version control
The system must track which requirements have been modified and only send dirty (changed) requirements to the mapper for processing
The hook must validate API access before proceeding with analysis and exit non-zero to block the commit if API authentication fails
The system must analyze the staged diff and Claude Code conversation log using the unified conversation reading system
The system must write pending decisions to branch-specific decision log files with filesystem-safe path sanitization
The system must print a machine-readable JSON summary of pending decisions to stdout and exit non-zero when pending decisions exist
The system must include explicit safeguards to never approve, reject, or edit decisions on the user's behalf
Users must be able to approve multiple pending decisions efficiently using the '--all' flag with the approve command
The commit must only land when there are zero pending decisions
The system must raise a custom PlumbAuthError exception when API authentication fails that alerts the user and provides clear instructions
API key validation must be implemented as a separate validate_api_access function that is called before all LLM operations
The system must support environment variable management using .env files with python-dotenv dependency
The system must use gitignore-style patterns for ignore functionality, with default patterns used when no .plumbignore file exists
The conversation parser must handle Claude Code's type/message schema format and convert tool usage blocks to text format '[tool: Name] description'
The system must implement intelligent deduplication by first running Jaccard similarity filtering, then applying LLM deduplication as a second pass when 2 or more candidates remain
The system must generate complete and runnable tests rather than stub files with TODO comments
The test generator must produce fully functional test code that can be executed immediately and only run if tests don't already exist
The system must provide read_all_decisions() as the primary API function for accessing decisions across all branches
The system must implement spec updates using a single LLM call per spec file with an output schema containing section_updates and new_sections
The legacy decisions path function must be maintained for migration detection purposes while the primary system operates on branch-specific decision logs
The system must analyze the staged diff and read Claude Code's native session files directly from auto-detected paths
The system must extract prescriptive choices while excluding observations and diagnostics
The system must implement within-batch similarity deduplication to prevent duplicate decisions in the same batch
The system must use both last_commit datetime and last_extracted_at timestamp as cutoffs, taking the later of the two to determine which conversations to process
The system must pass current decisions and recent decisions from the last couple commits to the deduplication pass using a context window of 200 decisions
The system must prioritize approved/synced decisions over recent ones in the comparison set
The system must use exact deduplication and LLM-based semantic deduplication with groq/openai/gpt-oss-120b configured for both decision_deduplicator and question_synthesizer
The 'plumb init' command must check that the current directory is a git repository and exit with an error if not
The 'plumb init' command must create the '.plumb/' directory if it does not exist
The 'plumb init' command must prompt the user interactively to provide paths to spec files and test files, validating that the paths exist
The 'plumb init' command must write '.plumb/config.json' with the provided paths
The 'plumb init' command must create a '.plumbignore' file in the project root if it does not exist
The 'plumb init' command must install the git pre-commit hook by writing a script to '.git/hooks/pre-commit' that calls 'plumb hook' and sets the script as executable
The 'plumb init' command must install the Claude Code skill locally by copying 'plumb/skill/SKILL.md' to '.claude/skills/plumb/SKILL.md' in the project root
The 'plumb init' command must append a Plumb status block to 'CLAUDE.md' at the project root, creating 'CLAUDE.md' if it does not exist
The 'plumb init' command must run 'plumb parse-spec' to do an initial parse of the spec into requirements
The 'plumb init' command must print a confirmation summary to the terminal, including confirmation that the skill was installed
The 'plumb hook' command must read '.plumb/config.json' and exit 0 silently if not found
The 'plumb hook' command must get the current staged diff via 'git diff --cached'
The 'plumb hook' command must get the current branch name
The 'plumb hook' command must detect amends by comparing the HEAD commit's parent SHA to last_commit
The 'plumb hook' command must detect broken references by checking all SHAs in decisions.jsonl against git history
The 'plumb hook' command must run the Diff Analysis DSPy program on the staged diff
The 'plumb hook' command must attempt to locate and read the Claude Code conversation log
The 'plumb hook' command must filter out decisions marked as non-spec-relevant during extraction
The 'plumb hook' command must merge and deduplicate decisions across chunks using DuckDB for cross-shard queries
The 'plumb hook' command must run Question Synthesizer for each decision with no associated question
The 'plumb hook' command must write all new decisions with status 'pending' to decisions.jsonl
The 'plumb hook' command must run 'plumb parse-spec' to update requirements cache for any modified spec files
The 'plumb hook' command must check whether it is running in a TTY or as a subprocess when pending decisions exist
The 'plumb hook' command must print human-readable summary in TTY mode and machine-readable JSON in non-TTY mode
The 'plumb hook' command must exit non-zero when pending decisions exist, aborting the commit
The 'plumb hook' command must run 'plumb coverage' and update config when no pending decisions exist, then exit 0
The hook must never exit non-zero due to an internal Plumb error
The 'plumb hook --dry-run' command must run the full hook analysis on staged changes but not write to decisions.jsonl and always exit 0
The 'plumb diff' command must preview what Plumb will capture from currently staged changes in read-only mode
The 'plumb diff' command must read staged changes via 'git diff --cached'
The 'plumb diff' command must run Diff Analysis on the staged diff
The 'plumb diff' command must read and chunk the conversation log if available
The 'plumb diff' command must run Decision Extraction per chunk
The 'plumb diff' command must print a preview to the terminal with summary and estimated decisions
The 'plumb diff' command must make no writes to '.plumb/'
The 'plumb review' command must provide interactive review of pending decisions for terminal use
The 'plumb review' command must read '.plumb/decisions.jsonl' and filter for status == 'pending'
The 'plumb review' command must accept an optional '--branch <name>' flag to filter by branch
The 'plumb review' command must print 'No pending decisions.' and exit 0 if no pending decisions exist
The 'plumb review' command must display decision details and prompt the user for approve, reject, edit, or skip
The 'plumb review' command must run 'plumb sync' for all approved/edited decisions after all decisions are resolved
The 'plumb review' command must default spec_relevant field to True when LLM classification is uncertain
The 'plumb approve <id>' command must approve a single decision by ID and update its status to 'approved' in decisions.jsonl
The 'plumb approve <id>' command must run 'plumb sync' for that decision only
The 'plumb reject <id>' command must reject a single decision by ID and update its status to 'rejected' with recorded reason
The 'plumb reject <id>' command must not modify code or spec
The 'plumb edit <id>' command must replace the decision text for a given decision ID with user-provided text
The 'plumb edit <id>' command must update status to 'edited' and run 'plumb sync' for that decision only
The 'plumb modify <id>' command must modify the staged code to satisfy a rejected decision
The 'plumb modify <id>' command must read the decision object and verify status == 'rejected'
The 'plumb modify <id>' command must read the staged diff and call the Claude API with modification instructions
The 'plumb modify <id>' command must apply the proposed modification to the staged files
The 'plumb modify <id>' command must run pytest on the test suite to verify tests still pass after modification
The 'plumb modify <id>' command must stage modifications only if tests pass and update decision status accordingly
The 'plumb modify <id>' command must return machine-readable JSON result in non-TTY mode
Plumb must never commit modifications, only stage them
The 'plumb sync' command must update the spec and tests to reflect all approved and edited decisions
The 'plumb sync' command must read decisions with status 'approved' or 'edited' that have not yet been synced
The 'plumb sync' command must run WholeFileSpecUpdater for each spec file processing all decisions together
The 'plumb sync' command must run OutlineMerger if new_sections is non-empty to determine proper positioning
The 'plumb sync' command must write updated spec files to disk using temp file → rename
The 'plumb sync' command must run Test Generator to generate organized tests for uncovered requirements
The 'plumb sync' command must write generated tests to test_generated.py using temp file → rename
The 'plumb sync' command must run 'plumb parse-spec' to re-cache requirements
The 'plumb sync' command must set synced_at on each processed decision
The 'plumb sync' command must print a summary of spec sections updated and test files created
The 'plumb parse-spec' command must parse all spec markdown files into an explicit list of requirements and cache them
The 'plumb parse-spec' command must read all markdown files in spec_paths from config.json
The 'plumb parse-spec' command must run Requirement Parser for each file or paragraph block
The 'plumb parse-spec' command must assign each requirement a stable ID based on a hash of its content
The 'plumb parse-spec' command must write results to '.plumb/requirements.json'
The 'plumb parse-spec' command must preserve existing metadata for requirements with matching hashes
The 'plumb parse-spec' command must use regex patterns to extract requirement IDs from test files for coverage mapping
The 'plumb parse-spec' command must process all requirements and AST summaries through CodeCoverageMapper in batch operations
The 'plumb parse-spec' command must implement granular per-file hashing with structured v2 cache format
The 'plumb parse-spec' command must extract source file paths from evidence strings using pattern matching
The 'plumb coverage' command must report coverage across all three dimensions: code, spec-to-test, and spec-to-code
The 'plumb coverage' command must run 'pytest --cov' and parse output for code coverage
The 'plumb coverage' command must check whether tests reference or map to each requirement for spec-to-test coverage
The 'plumb coverage' command must check whether each requirement has a corresponding implementation for spec-to-code coverage
The 'plumb coverage' command must print a formatted table using rich
The 'plumb coverage' command must use LLM calls to refresh the requirements cache for accurate coverage analysis
The 'plumb coverage' command must measure and report coverage improvement achieved by marker injection
The 'plumb status' command must print a human-readable summary including tracked files, requirements, tests, pending decisions, broken references, last sync commit, and coverage summary
Plumb must ship with a Claude Code skill file at 'plumb/skill/SKILL.md' that is copied to '.claude/SKILL.md' in the project root during init
The skill file must provide structured guidance for AI-assisted development workflow
The skill file must teach Claude Code the Plumb workflow and provide machine-readable protocol for parsing hook output
The system must support reading Claude Code native session files with JSONL parsing from the '~/.claude/projects/' directory structure
The '.claude/' directory and 'SKILL.md' should be committed to version control so all contributors get the skill automatically
'plumb init' must append a specific Plumb status block to 'CLAUDE.md' delimited by comment markers
Plumb must be configurable in '.plumb/config.json' under 'claude_log_path'
If Claude log path is not set, Plumb must attempt to auto-detect using common Claude Code log locations
If Claude log is not found, Plumb must skip conversation analysis and note 'conversation_available': false
Plumb must read only turns recorded after the 'last_commit' timestamp in config.json
Session files must be pre-filtered by modification time to avoid reading old files
The conversation parser must handle the native session JSONL format with type/message schema used by Claude Code
Chunking must be performed in conversation.py before any DSPy program is called with no LLM involvement
The primary unit for chunking must be user turn: one user message plus all following assistant turns up to the next user message
If a chunk exceeds 6,000 tokens, it must be split at tool call boundaries or at the midpoint of the largest assistant turn
Chunking must prepend the final assistant turn of the previous chunk as a header to the next chunk
Before chunking, tool result turns longer than 500 tokens that appear to be raw file reads must be replaced with '[file read: <filename>]'
Assistant messages that span multiple JSONL entries must be properly parsed and reassembled
DecisionExtractor must be called once per chunk with diff_summary passed identically to every call
Results must be merged after all chunks with near-duplicate decisions collapsed into one, preserving the earliest chunk_index
On amends, the pre-commit hook must compare HEAD's parent SHA to last_commit and delete decisions where commit_sha == last_commit before re-running analysis
On every hook run, all stored SHAs must be checked against git history and unreachable SHAs flagged 'ref_status': 'broken'
Plumb must not attempt to re-map decisions to new SHAs after a rebase
Users must review and re-resolve broken-reference decisions manually
The decision log must be append-only with existing lines never modified in place
Status updates must be written as new lines with the same id, with the latest line for a given id being canonical
DiffAnalyzer must take raw unified diff string as input and output list of change summaries with files_changed, summary, and change_type
DiffAnalyzer must group related changes into logical units and not invent meaning
DecisionExtractor must take chunk and diff_summary as input and output list of decision objects with question, decision, made_by, related_diff_summary, and confidence
DecisionExtractor must extract explicit and implicit decisions but not extract trivial decisions like variable naming or import ordering
QuestionSynthesizer must take a decision object with no associated question as input and output a plain-English question framing the decision
RequirementParser must take markdown string as input and output list of requirement objects with text and ambiguous fields
RequirementParser must use rules: atomic statements, active voice, no duplicates, with vague statements flagged ambiguous: true
SpecUpdater must take spec_section and decision as input and output updated markdown for that section
SpecUpdater must capture the result of decision as natural requirement with no reference to the decision itself and preserve existing formatting
TestGenerator must take requirements, existing_tests, and code_context as input and output runnable pytest test code as a Python string
TestGenerator must create one function per requirement with descriptive names, real assertions against actual code, no pytest.skip() or TODO comments, and no overwriting existing tests
CodeModifier must be implemented as Claude API call (not DSPy) for open-ended code modification
CodeModifier must take staged diff, rejected decision, rejection reason, and current spec as input and output modified file contents
All CLI commands must fail gracefully with clear error message if config.json is missing or malformed
All DSPy programs must retry on LLM failure (max 2 retries) then raise PlumbInferenceError with human-readable message
The git hook must never exit non-zero due to an internal Plumb error
File writes must use temp file → rename to avoid partial writes
If conversation log is unavailable, Plumb must continue with diff-only analysis (not an error)
If plumb modify test run fails, Plumb must not stage the modification and update decision status to rejected_manual
The plumb status command must operate in cache-only mode to avoid making LLM calls
The plumb coverage command must display progress using an in-place updating spinner, then print the final results table
Session file reading must be optimized by pre-filtering based on file modification times to skip files that cannot contain relevant conversations
Plumb must be tested with pytest with 80% coverage minimum for v0.1.0
cli.py must be tested for all commands running without error given valid inputs and per-decision commands updating decisions.jsonl correctly
decision_log.py must be tested for read/write/filter/dedup on .jsonl and latest-line-wins logic for status updates
git_hook.py must be tested for hook producing correct pending decisions, amend detection, and TTY vs non-TTY output formats
conversation.py must be tested for correct chunk boundaries, overlap, noise reduction, metadata, and oversized chunk splitting
programs/ must be tested for each DSPy program producing correctly structured output given fixture inputs
coverage_reporter.py must be tested for correct calculations given mock pytest output
sync.py must be tested for spec and test files updated correctly given approved decisions with no partial writes
Plumb must support loading environment variables from .env files to manage configuration settings
The plumb init command must create a .env file in the project directory to facilitate environment-based configuration management
The system must support ANTHROPIC_API_KEY configuration through both environment variables and .env files
Tests must be linked to requirements through requirement ID comments to establish traceability between test functions and specific requirements
All tests must be organized in the tests/ directory with specific test files for each component
Tests without requirement links must be treated as sync violations that indicate either missing requirements or unnecessary tests
The system must provide a 'check' command as an alias for manual decision scanning
Generated cache and coverage files must be excluded from commits
Plumb must handle conversations that span multiple Claude Code session files by reading and merging them chronologically
The sync command must provide progress indicators with status updates to give users feedback during operation
The workflow must require an explicit sync step after approving decisions, with staging of sync output before re-committing changes
The system must use a branch-sharded structure for decision logs
The system must provide CLI commands for merging decision logs from different branches
Users must be able to migrate existing decision logs from the old monolithic format to the new branch-sharded layout through dedicated CLI commands
The plumb merge-decisions <branch> command must allow users to merge decisions from feature branches back to main
A find_decision_branch() function must help locate which branch file contains a specific decision ID
The WholeFileSpecUpdater must take full spec content and decisions as input, and output section_updates for existing sections and new_sections for brand new sections
The WholeFileSpecUpdater must accept rewriting whole sections instead of making surgical edits for markdown specs
An OutlineMerger component must handle structural changes when new sections need to be added or content needs reordering
The system must include helper functions for DuckDB result type conversion, using _clean_duckdb_row() and _to_python_native() functions
Comprehensive test coverage must be implemented for migration and merge functionality, including edge cases and error conditions
DSPy programs must support program-specific model configuration through a model configuration array in config.json
Pattern parsing logic must be modularized into separate functions for reusability across programs, including an extract_outline function
The system must implement multi-stage deduplication using exact matching, Jaccard similarity, and LLM semantic analysis to catch different types of duplicates
Only prescriptive choices that affect system design, behavior, architecture, or data models must be captured as decisions
Process observations, tooling choices, and diagnostic findings must be filtered out
Spec updates must use a search-and-replace approach with edits containing old_text/new_text pairs rather than full section replacement
Section updates must use exact header matching first, then fall back to normalized matching for minor variations in header formatting
The status display must include a visual bar chart representation for coverage statistics
The status display must use Rich's Status spinner that updates in-place for progress indication
After commit completion, the post-commit hook must clear the last_extracted_at timestamp to reset the filter for future extractions
The system must include comprehensive documentation for the Plumb skill with detailed instructions for managing spec/test/code alignment and decision review processes
The system must use DSPy's context manager with Haiku LM for deduplication while preserving Sonnet for other operations
The deduplication process must prioritize approved and synced decisions over recent ones in the comparison set and use an expanded context window of 200 decisions instead of 50
Test generation must use increased context limits to accommodate complete test code
The review command must pre-compute decision branches to optimize performance and ensure correct shard targeting for updates
Users must be able to migrate from the monolithic decision file to the sharded layout using the plumb migrate command
The system must keep edit operations simple without complex operation sets and accept the risk of unintended edits in exchange for performance gains
Structural reasoning for section placement must be kept separate from content generation to maintain clarity
Source summaries must be structured as per-file mappings to enable granular tracking
All documentation files including SKILL files and CLAUDE.md must be kept consistent with the same sync workflow requirements
The system must draft commit messages after decision review and include the list of approved decisions
When multiple session files exist, the system must use the most recent session file by modification time or match the current session ID for session selection
The conversation log reader must work seamlessly across different repositories without requiring additional user setup or configuration overhead
When parsing session files, the system must skip assistant entries that contain 'thinking' blocks, have 'isSidechain: true', or have 'isMeta: true'
Rejected decisions must be marked with 'ignored' status rather than being deleted from the system
All CLI operations must require explicit user commands and the system shall not perform automatic synchronization or background operations without direct user instruction
The sync operation must identify and use the correct decision branch when marking decisions as synced

When handling decision conflicts, the system must use opposite ordering rules: for duplicate decisions, keep the earlier one (lower index); for countermanded decisions, keep the later one (higher index)
The system must use section-header-keyed full replacement instead of search-and-replace for markdown spec editing
